{{Infobox concept
| name = Fate
| introduced = Skysworn
}}

''See also: [[Fate Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Fate is a fundamental force or tendency that dictates the flow of events and potential outcomes within the world. It can be visualized as a collection of strands or branches representing various possibilities, some of which are more probable than others. While it often functions as a natural progression of cause and effect, certain powerful entities can perceive, manipulate, or even disrupt its flow. Some individuals, such as [[Monarchs]] and certain [[Abidan]] members like [[Judges]], possess the ability to look into Fate to see potential futures, though these visions can be obscured by chaos, corruption, or intentional concealment. The nature of Fate is not entirely fixed; it can be influenced by the intentions of individuals, the presence of chaotic beings like [[the Mad King]] or [[Dreadgods]], and even specific weapons designed to eliminate certain possibilities. While some view Fate as an inevitable destiny or the will of the heavens, others see it as a set of tendencies that can be altered through decisive action. Certain specialized groups, such as the [[Abidan]] [[Hounds]], attempt to monitor these flows, though their ability to predict the future is limited, and they may find themselves unable to read the destinies of those who regularly diverge from established paths.}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Purple formations in eyes can see [[Fate]].}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Executors]] could interfere in [[Fate]] because they were not bound to the [[Eledari Pact]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Fate]] has been accelerated to bring a specific day sooner than expected.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[The Mad King]]'s presence warps [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A path shone in [[Fate]].}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Elder Whisper]] occasionally experiences fleeting glimpses of Fate during his dreams. This cosmic force is later perceived as being disrupted by [[Eithan]]. During a conflict, [[Makiel]] contended that any attempt to save the world would constitute a deviation from Fate. Ultimately, it is revealed that [[Mercy]]'s friends only managed to survive due to its influence, and [[Eithan]] possesses the ability to see and arrange it.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Elder Whisper]] can sometimes catch glimpses of [[Fate]] in his dreams.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] senses a disruption in [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Makiel]] argued that saving the world would be a deviation from [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Mercy]]'s friends only survived because of [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] can see and arrange [[Fate]].}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Fate is a conceptual force that governs the destiny and various branches of worlds, composed of structures that are capable of dissolving. The flows of this concept bend in response to the actions taken by [[Ozriel]], and it can be further influenced by the intentions of others. While [[Daruman]] possesses the ability to twist Fate, [[Suriel]] can scan its patterns to read upcoming events.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Fate]] consists of branches that can dissolve.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Fate]]'s flows bend according to [[Ozriel]]'s actions.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Fate]] is a concept that governs the destiny and branches of worlds.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Daruman]] can twist [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Intentions influence [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Suriel]] can scan [[Fate]] to read events.}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|During the unfolding events, [[Suriel]]'s runic eyes were capable of perceiving Fate as it was being woven. This [[essence]] was seen to split and twist by the eyes of [[Makiel]], while the [[Penance arrow]] later thundered through both space and Fate. The oracle codex was utilized to check the state of Fate, revealing its true flow. Finally, [[Malice]] gazed into the shadows cast by Fate.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Suriel]]'s runic eyes can see the [[Fate]] being woven.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Makiel]] saw [[Fate]] split and twist.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Penance arrow]] thundered through space and [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The oracle codex checked [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The oracle codex shows the real flow of [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Malice]] gazed into the shadows of [[Fate]].}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Fate is a phenomenon that can be physically touched through the [[Way]]. It offers projections of future events, which [[Dross]] is capable of glimpsing. However, reading these projections can result in inaccuracies. Currently, Fate has not yet reached a state of stability, and the [[OTEP]] possesses no more access to it than any other [[Presence]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fate]] is something that can be touched through the [[Way]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fate]] provides projections of future events.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dross]] can glimpse [[Fate]].}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Reading]] [[Fate]] can lead to being wrong.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fate]] has yet to stabilize.}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[OTEP]] has no greater access to [[Fate]] than any other [[Presence]].}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|“Fate is not the future. What is destined to occur does not always occur.”<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11">''Unsouled'', chapter 11</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle concepts]]
[[Category:Terminology]]
